behavior_summary: U8P_BRANCHES::look is a U8P_BRANCHES::look-type process that runs when the player examines the BRANCHES object.
When invoked it displays a single short description/bark: "tree " (note the trailing space in the source).
After displaying the bark the process yields/suspends, ending the short-description handling. There are no menus, branches, variable reads/writes, or external calls involved.
notable_control_flow: Linear: display one Bark node then suspend/yield; no conditional branches or menu flow.
No internal or external calls are made; the routine's effect is purely UI text emission and process suspension.
open_questions: Is the trailing space in the bark text "tree " intentional (formatting for concatenation) or an artifact?
Does the 'suspend' imply a short timed display managed by the engine or simply the end of the U8P_BRANCHES::look process?
Is this U8P_BRANCHES::look handler ever invoked by area triggers (enterFastArea/leaveFastArea) in other contexts, or only by direct 'U8P_BRANCHES::look' player action?

main_function.name: U8P_BRANCHES::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short description for BRANCHES when the player examines it, then yield/end the U8P_BRANCHES::look process.
entry_conditions: Player uses the 'U8P_BRANCHES::look' (examine) action on the BRANCHES object.
Invoked by the engine's U8P_BRANCHES::look-type process dispatch (processType 0x0208) when the object is inspected or focused.

function_map.name: U8P_BRANCHES::look
function_map.role: Handle object examination by showing a short descriptive bark and suspending the U8P_BRANCHES::look process.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_BRANCHES::look is a U8P_BRANCHES::look-type process that runs when the player examines the BRANCHES object.
